gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An immutable persistent membership set (value-semantic; spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Iteration order is **insertion order** (first insertion of each element determines"] # [doc = " position). This is a documented, stable property — never an exposed hash-bucket order."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG: §7-Q1"] # [doc = " Insertion-order is chosen as the deterministic floor; M-501 may ratify a different"] # [doc = " default. The property tests check the order-stability invariant and will catch regressions."] # [derive (Debug , Clone)] pub struct Set < E > { # [doc = " Elements in insertion order."] order : Vec < E > , # [doc = " Element → index in `order` (non-identity bucketing hash — not std.content)."] index : HashMap < E , usize > , }

gaps.snippet: impl < E : Clone + Eq + std :: hash :: Hash > Set < E > { # [doc = " An empty `Set`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn empty () -> Self { Set { order : Vec :: new () , index : HashMap :: new () , } } # [doc = " Construct a `Set` from a slice of elements (insertion order = slice order;"] # [doc = " duplicates take the first occurrence's position)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn from_slice (elems : & [E]) -> Self { let mut s = Set :: empty () ; for e in elems { s = s . insert (e . clone ()) ; } s } # [doc = " The number of elements."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn len (& self) -> usize { self . order . len () } # [doc = " `true` iff the set is empty."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn is_empty (& self) -> bool { self . order . is_empty () } # [doc = " `true` iff `x` is in the set (C1 — total, never a partial result)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn contains (& self , x : & E) -> bool { self . index . contains_key (x) } # [doc = " Insert `x`, returning a **new** `Set`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " If `x` is already present, the new set has the same contents (idempotent)."] # [doc = " The receiver is not modified (C4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn insert (& self , x : E) -> Self { if self . index . contains_key (& x) { self . clone () } else { let mut new_set = self . clone () ; let i = new_set . order . len () ; new_set . order . push (x . clone ()) ; new_set . index . insert (x , i) ; new_set } } # [doc = " Remove `x`, returning a **new** `Set`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " If `x` is absent, the new set has the same contents (\"no-op-returning-new\""] # [doc = " guarantee from spec §3). The receiver is not modified (C4). No error is returned"] # [doc = " for a missing element (C1 — the absence of a non-member is not an error condition)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn remove (& self , x : & E) -> Self { if let Some (& i) = self . index . get (x) { let mut new_order : Vec < E > = Vec :: with_capacity (self . order . len () - 1) ; let mut new_index : HashMap < E , usize > = HashMap :: with_capacity (self . order . len () - 1) ; for (j , e) in self . order . iter () . enumerate () { if j != i { let new_i = new_order . len () ; new_order . push (e . clone ()) ; new_index . insert (e . clone () , new_i) ; } } Set { order : new_order , index : new_index , } } else { self . clone () } } # [doc = " Union: all elements from `self` then any new elements from `other`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Order: `self` elements first (in their insertion order), then elements that are in"] # [doc = " `other` but not in `self` (in `other`'s insertion order). This is documented and"] # [doc = " deterministic — never an exposed hash-bucket order."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN (C3)"] # [doc = " The result order (self first, then other-only) is the inspectable artifact."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn union (& self , other : & Self) -> Self { let mut result = self . clone () ; for e in & other . order { result = result . insert (e . clone ()) ; } result } # [doc = " Intersection: elements present in **both** `self` and `other`, in `self`'s"] # [doc = " insertion order."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Order: the order of elements in `self` is preserved for those that are also in"] # [doc = " `other`. Documented and deterministic."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN (C3)"] # [doc = " The result order (self's order, filtered) is the inspectable artifact."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn intersection (& self , other : & Self) -> Self { let mut result = Set :: empty () ; for e in & self . order { if other . contains (e) { result = result . insert (e . clone ()) ; } } result } # [doc = " Difference: elements in `self` that are **not** in `other`, in `self`'s insertion"] # [doc = " order."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Order: self's insertion order, filtered to elements absent from `other`. Documented"] # [doc = " and deterministic."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN (C3)"] # [doc = " The result order (self's order, filtered) is the inspectable artifact."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn difference (& self , other : & Self) -> Self { let mut result = Set :: empty () ; for e in & self . order { if ! other . contains (e) { result = result . insert (e . clone ()) ; } } result } # [doc = " Iterate elements in **insertion order** (documented order, no silent reorder)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the documented-order promise (the no-silent-reorder crux). The order is"] # [doc = " a property of the type, not of the build or hash seed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn foldable (& self) -> & [E] { & self . order } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; fn set_abc () -> Set < & 'static str > { Set :: from_slice (& ["a" , "b" , "c"]) } # [doc = " `insert` returns a new `Set`; original is unchanged (C4)."] # [test] fn insert_returns_new_set_original_unchanged () { let s = Set :: empty () ; let s2 = s . insert ("x") ; assert ! (s . is_empty () , "original must be unchanged after insert (C4)") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . len () , 1) ; } # [doc = " `remove` returns a new `Set`; original is unchanged (C4)."] # [test] fn remove_returns_new_set_original_unchanged () { let s = set_abc () ; let s2 = s . remove (& "b") ; assert_eq ! (s . len () , 3 , "original must be unchanged after remove (C4)") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . len () , 2) ; } # [doc = " `insert` is idempotent: re-inserting a present element returns the same contents."] # [test] fn insert_is_idempotent () { let s = set_abc () ; let s2 = s . insert ("a") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . len () , 3 , "idempotent insert must not grow the set") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . foldable () , s . foldable () , "idempotent insert must not change order") ; } # [doc = " `remove` is idempotent on absent elements: returns new set with same contents (C1)."] # [test] fn remove_absent_element_returns_same_contents () { let s = set_abc () ; let s2 = s . remove (& "z") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . len () , 3 , "absent remove must not shrink the set") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . foldable () , s . foldable ()) ; } # [doc = " `contains` is total and never misses present elements."] # [test] fn contains_is_total_and_accurate () { let s = set_abc () ; assert ! (s . contains (& "a")) ; assert ! (s . contains (& "b")) ; assert ! (s . contains (& "c")) ; assert ! (! s . contains (& "z") , "C1: honest absence") ; } # [doc = " `foldable` walks in insertion order."] # [test] fn foldable_walks_in_insertion_order () { let s = set_abc () ; assert_eq ! (s . foldable () , & ["a" , "b" , "c"] , "foldable must walk in insertion order (spec §3)") ; } # [doc = " Inserting elements in a non-sorted order preserves that order in `foldable`."] # [test] fn insertion_order_preserved_regardless_of_element_hash () { let s = Set :: from_slice (& ["c" , "a" , "b"]) ; assert_eq ! (s . foldable () , & ["c" , "a" , "b"] , "foldable must walk in INSERTION order, not hash/alpha order (no silent reorder)") ; } # [doc = " `union`: self-elements first, then other-only elements."] # [test] fn union_order_self_first_then_other_only () { let a = Set :: from_slice (& [1 , 2 , 3]) ; let b = Set :: from_slice (& [3 , 4 , 5]) ; let u = a . union (& b) ; assert_eq ! (u . foldable () , & [1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5]) ; } # [doc = " `intersection`: self's order, filtered to elements also in other."] # [test] fn intersection_order_is_self_filtered () { let a = Set :: from_slice (& [1 , 2 , 3 , 4]) ; let b = Set :: from_slice (& [4 , 3 , 5]) ; let i = a . intersection (& b) ; assert_eq ! (i . foldable () , & [3 , 4]) ; } # [doc = " `difference`: self's order, filtered to elements not in other."] # [test] fn difference_order_is_self_filtered () { let a = Set :: from_slice (& [1 , 2 , 3 , 4]) ; let b = Set :: from_slice (& [3 , 4 , 5]) ; let d = a . difference (& b) ; assert_eq ! (d . foldable () , & [1 , 2]) ; } # [doc = " union is commutative in *content* but NOT necessarily in *order*."] # [test] fn union_content_is_commutative_but_order_may_differ () { let a = Set :: from_slice (& [1 , 2]) ; let b = Set :: from_slice (& [2 , 3]) ; let u_ab = a . union (& b) ; let u_ba = b . union (& a) ; assert ! (u_ab . contains (& 1)) ; assert ! (u_ab . contains (& 2)) ; assert ! (u_ab . contains (& 3)) ; assert ! (u_ba . contains (& 1)) ; assert ! (u_ba . contains (& 2)) ; assert ! (u_ba . contains (& 3)) ; assert_eq ! (u_ab . foldable () , & [1 , 2 , 3]) ; assert_eq ! (u_ba . foldable () , & [2 , 3 , 1]) ; } # [doc = " Property: two sets with the same elements inserted in the same order yield the"] # [doc = " same `foldable` sequence."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Spec §4 §7-Q2: `Declared` invariant → promoted to `Empirical` by this test (VR-5)."] # [test] fn equal_contents_same_order_yield_same_foldable () { let s1 = Set :: from_slice (& [10 , 20 , 30]) ; let s2 = Set :: from_slice (& [10 , 20 , 30]) ; assert_eq ! (s1 . foldable () , s2 . foldable () , "same contents + same insertion order must yield same foldable (no silent reorder)") ; } # [doc = " Property: after remove, the remaining elements keep their relative insertion order."] # [test] fn remove_preserves_relative_order_of_remaining_elements () { let s = set_abc () ; let s2 = s . remove (& "b") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . foldable () , & ["a" , "c"] , "remove must preserve relative insertion order of remaining elements (no silent reorder)") ; } # [doc = " Property: insert then remove round-trips (element absent after removal)."] # [test] fn insert_remove_round_trip () { let s = set_abc () ; let s2 = s . insert ("d") ; assert ! (s2 . contains (& "d")) ; let s3 = s2 . remove (& "d") ; assert ! (! s3 . contains (& "d")) ; assert_eq ! (s3 . len () , s . len () , "round-trip must restore original length") ; assert_eq ! (s3 . foldable () , s . foldable () , "round-trip must restore original order") ; } }
